---
title: 'Parables | Stories | Word Pictures'
date: '2015-04-20T12:40:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6706601748365244481'
blogger_filename: '/2015/04/parables-stories-word-pictures.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Perspective'
---

<br /><br /><br /><h4>Teaching Through Stories</h4><br /><br />So yesterday @GatewayPeople (<a class="g-profile" href="https://plus.google.com/116722015803706563630" target="_blank">+gatewaychurchtv</a>&nbsp;) Pastor Robert Morris taught about Jesus "The Teacher".<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="margin-bottom: 1em;"><a href="http://gatewaypeople.com/ministries/life/events/jesus-a-gateway-series/service/2015/04/18/the-teacher" target="_blank">The Teacher</a> | Service | 7 | Jesus | A Gateway Series | Robert Morris | 28:44 | 04/18/2015<br />Pastor Robert continues the&nbsp;<em>Jesus&nbsp;</em>series with a message titled, â€œThe Teacher,â€ where he explains how Jesus is a great teacher and why His teachings were so compelling and powerful.&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq">Matthew 13:54, 22:33; John 7:32, 45â€“46 , Matthew 7:28â€“29&nbsp;</blockquote><br /><br />He mentioned how nearly all of Jesus messages were based in Stories and Word Pictures. This was in comparison to the teachers of the day, who taught dry, disjointed, rule-based teachings. One person noted that the volumes of teaching from Israel's Rabbi's were the longest, drying, most boring, disjointed ramblings ever recorded. Yet, here was Jesus, and people would come from miles and miles, follow Him into deserted places, just to hear him speak.<br /><br />What caused them to want to listen? He taught in stories and word pictures. This has been a theme for me this year, as God has been dealing with me to delve into creative writing (fiction and possibly story based song writing (like some of Garth Brooks best stuff)).<br /><br />So if you start to hear/read more stories from me on this blog... this was your advanced notice that it was coming.<br /><br />#<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2014/10/be-still-and-be-led-by-his-still-small.html" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a>?<br /><br /><br />---<br /><h4>And now I leave you with a question:</h4><h4>&nbsp;</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>So... what is the most POWERFUL story you've ever heard/read?&nbsp;<a class="g-profile" href="https://plus.google.com/106337514196493406365" target="_blank"></a></b></blockquote><br /><b>Hit me up on Twitter&nbsp;<a href="https://twitter.com/darrellwolfe" target="_blank">@DarrellWolfe&nbsp;</a>or on Google&nbsp;<a class="g-profile" href="https://plus.google.com/106337514196493406365" target="_blank">+DarrellWolfe</a></b><br /><h4><g:plus action="share"></g:plus></h4><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><div><ul><li><span style="font-family: Georgia,&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/got-jesus.html" target="_blank">Got Jesus?</a></span></li><li><span style="font-family: Georgia,&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">About This Blog, and Darrell.&nbsp;</a></span></li><li><span style="font-family: Georgia,&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/books-resources.html" target="_blank">Book Suggestions</a></span></li><li><span style="font-family: Georgia,&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/blog-page_27.html" target="_blank">Suggestions for other: Bloggers, Authors, Ministries, Thought Leaders, and More</a></span></li><li><span style="font-family: Georgia,&quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/my-tool-box.html" target="_blank">My Tool Box: suggestions for some of the tools I've used in my journey.</a></span></li></ul></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" height="232" width="320" /></a></div>
